sections.title: Talking to Fry Before talking to the Easter Bunny
Talking to Fry after talking to the Easter Bunny

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Fry, are you proceeding with the performance planning?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Oh yes, I'm perusing potential performances and am planning which parts will be picked.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perfect!

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Ah, hello [player name]! I've a fantastic performance for us all to practise, if you are ready for a rehearsal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, we'd better have a go!

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Alright then, let's assemble the troupe!

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Oh hang on, we don't want to ruin the surprise.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Easter Bunny! There's more fancy carrot juice at the bar!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Ooooh! Outta my way!

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Rightio then, let's get to it.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Glanton! Rinder! Eggus! It's time!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Time fer what?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Time for the rehearsal! For the performance!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: I didn't think ye were serious about that!

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Well I am, so hurry along. You've all had a chance to familiarise yourselves with my script, yes?

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Oh yeah of course, I know it by heart. Hah!

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Perfect! Rinder?

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: What he said.

lines.speaker: Eggus Manus
lines.text: I also did not read it.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Oh you jokers, come on then! To the stage!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you there in a moment.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Wonderful, we're all here!

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Let's see what you've got then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This should be interesting.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: You first, Glanton!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Aw heck.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: From the top! First verse! And a one two three!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: Oh lordy, uh..

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: When the foeman bares his.. peels?

lines.speaker: Eggus Manus
lines.text: TARANTA-RA TARANTA-RA!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: We, dun dun dun, er...

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: I don't know this! I do not know this one!

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: For goodness sake.

lines.speaker: Rinder
lines.text: That wasn't very Easter-ey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, it wasn't.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Oh come on, it's an old standard!

lines.speaker: Glanton
lines.text: I don't even know what a foeman is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could we try something else?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Well I don't have anything to hand, but you're welcome to have a look backstage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Backstage eh? Alright.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello! I'm trying to cheer up the Easter Bunny, do you have any ideas?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Hmm, I have noticed his recent sour mood. Let me think for a moment...

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Aha! I have it! Let's put on a performance! That will cheer him up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A performance?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Yes! He's always enjoyed a good show.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I suppose that could work...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you perform something soon?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Well, I'll need some time to find something appropriate to perform.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll come back later when you've worked something out.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Of course, I'll need some performers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely you don't mean me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You do mean me.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Naturally! I'll talk to some other people too and get a good troupe together!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, as long as it's not just me.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Of course not, you don't look deserving of a solo to me! Now run along, I shall begin my creative process.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you later then.

sections.title: Talking to Fry again before checking the backstage

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Have you found anything useful backstage yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Well get to it then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In my own time.

sections.title: Talking to Fry after checking backstage

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Have you found anything useful backstage yet?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Have you found anything useful backstage yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found some old costumes we can use.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Oh that's great, but we'll need more than just costumes to wow the audience.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: We need some material to perform!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: True, I'll have another look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found an old script, but I haven't read it yet.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Well perhaps you should read it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose I should.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found an old script and some carrot costumes!

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: An old script eh? Is it any good?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would show you but I seem to have misplaced it, I'll go and find it again.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: You silly sausage!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You tell me, here have a look. It seems like it's all out of order.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Well this is very interesting! Though I'm not sure how it would have been put together originally.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: In any case, it's definitely got the spirit of Easter written into it. Carrot costumes should suit this well.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: I think we can use this, though the page order will need reviewing. Can you work out something good?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can give it a go if you wash those disgusting carrot costumes.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Wonderful, bring it to me once you're done and we shall get cracking! In the meantime, I'll get those costumes cleaned.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: How is that script looking? Have you sorted it out yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It looks good to me, let's put on the performance!

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Wonderful! I shall inform our colleagues, and fetch the Easter Bunny.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you after the show.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: That was amazing!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: So much Easter energy! Wow!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: That dance was amazing! Well done. I feel so... Invigorated!

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: How could I ever lose my Easter spirit?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sometimes you just need a break.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: That's true. Thanks, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad you're feeling better.

lines.speaker: Easter Bunny
lines.text: Lawks! Look at the time, I should get back to Varrock to set up the Eggs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you up there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not finished yet.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Good job putting that script in order, I think that went well!

sections.title: Talking to Fry before finishing the rehearsal cutscene

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: I couldn't help overhearing that you're trying to cheer up the Easter Bunny, and I have an idea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: I am Fry, master of dramatics. Player of plays, singer of songs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A creative type eh?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Yes indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you here?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: I put on shows to entertain our patrons.

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: At least, I used to. We don't get many punters these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is that?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: I don't know, Easter isn't as big as it used to be I suppose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I had to crawl in here through a hole, do you think that's a factor?

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: It wasn't before, but I suppose it could be putting people off...

lines.speaker: Fry
lines.text: Hmm... Maybe we need a door...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe!
